gaps.why_it_matters: Understanding the funding structure and ownership is crucial for assessing financial stability, potential dilution, and strategic direction, especially for a company involved in manufacturing and brand management which can be capital intensive.

Credit ratings provide an independent assessment of the company's creditworthiness, which is important for understanding its financial risk and ability to access debt financing for expansion or operational needs in the manufacturing sector.

coverage.notes: The company is in the non-alcoholic beverage manufacturing and brand management sector. Most sections are populated, indicating good coverage. However, 'Ownership/Funding/Cap-Table or IPO Status' is only partially populated, and 'Credit ratings' is unavailable. Both are important for a manufacturing and brand-focused business. 'Ownership/Funding/Cap-Table or IPO Status' is important for understanding the financial backing and strategic control of the company. 'Credit ratings' are relevant for assessing financial risk and access to debt, which can be significant for a manufacturing operation.

company_header.name: Coca-Cola India
company_header.hq: Plot Nos. 1109- 1110 Village Pirangut, Taluka -Mulshi, Pirangut, Pune, Mulshi, Maharashtra, India, 412115

content.text: Coca-Cola India Private Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, USA, is a leading player in India's non-alcoholic beverage market. Incorporated in 1992, it primarily focuses on brand building, concentrate manufacturing, and marketing for a diverse portfolio including carbonated soft drinks (Coca-Cola, Thums Up, Sprite), juices (Maaza, Minute Maid), and water (Kinley). The company operates through a system that includes its largest bottling partner, Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Pvt Ltd (HCCBPL), which handles manufacturing, packaging, and distribution across a vast network of over a million retail outlets.

thesis.heading: Competitive Moat: Extensive Portfolio & Distribution
thesis.text: Coca-Cola India maintains a defensible market position through its extensive product portfolio, including global brands like Coca-Cola and Sprite, alongside strong local brands such as Thums Up and Limca, acquired via the 1993 purchase of Parle's beverage business for $60 million. This diverse offering is supported by a robust distribution network, leveraging its largest bottling partner, Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Private Limited (HCCB), ensuring widespread market penetration across India.

thesis.heading: Current Story: Strong Performance & Strategic Investment
thesis.text: The company demonstrates strong current momentum, evidenced by its FY 2024-25 revenue of INR 504,255.69 Lakhs and a robust PAT of INR 61,536.54 Lakhs, yielding an impressive ROE of 42.94%. This financial strength is complemented by a strategic commitment made in May 2024 by Coca-Cola India and its bottling partners to invest approximately USD 1 billion in India over the next three years (2024-2026) to expand manufacturing capacity and enhance distribution.

thesis.heading: Forward Opportunity: Market Growth & Catalytic Events
thesis.text: Significant forward opportunity is driven by the India Non-Alcoholic Beverages Market, projected to reach USD 34.71 Billion in 2025 and grow at a 7.94% CAGR (2026-2034). Key catalysts include the potential public listing (IPO) in India for Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings Pvt. Ltd. (HCCH) by 2027, planned expansion into new beverage categories (Q4 FY25), and strategic partnerships with e-commerce platforms (Q1 FY26) to enhance direct-to-consumer reach. Further, a strategic investment in HCCB by Jubilant Bhartia Group and Goldman Sachs Asset Management in July 2025 signals continued growth support.

flags.title: Volatile Profitability and Tight Liquidity in FY24
flags.description: Coca-Cola India experienced significant volatility in its profitability, with Profit After Tax (PAT) dropping from INR 72,243.06 Lakhs in FY 2022-23 to INR 42,017.26 Lakhs in FY 2023-24, before recovering to INR 61,536.54 Lakhs in FY 2024-25. Similarly, EBITDA saw a dip in FY 2023-24. Concurrently, the company's current ratio was tight at 1.03 in FY 2023-24, indicating potential short-term liquidity strain, although it improved slightly to 1.13 in FY 2024-25.

flags.title: Significant and Increasing Related Party Transactions (RPTs)
flags.description: Coca-Cola India engages in substantial related-party transactions. In FY 2024-25, the company paid INR 556.20 Crores in royalty to its ultimate parent, The Coca-Cola Company, USA, representing a 9.65% increase from the previous fiscal year. Additionally, significant transactions for sale/purchase of goods, discounts, and marketing support were conducted with Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Private Limited, a fellow subsidiary. While these are stated to be at 'arm's length,' the volume and increasing royalty payments warrant close monitoring for potential profit shifting or dependency.

flags.title: Unresolved Environmental and Social Liability (Plachimada Plant)
flags.description: The Coca-Cola bottling plant in Plachimada, Kerala, has been permanently closed since 2004-2005 due to widespread protests over groundwater exploitation and pollution. Despite a Kerala government committee recommending ₹216 crore (approximately $30 million) in compensation to affected villagers in 2010, a bill to establish a tribunal for this purpose was returned by the President in 2016, and no compensation has been paid to date. This represents a long-standing, unresolved social and environmental liability.

flags.title: Intensifying Competition in the Indian Beverage Market
flags.description: The Indian beverage market is experiencing intensifying competition. Coca-Cola's global president, John Murphy, acknowledged in January 2025 that the relaunch of Reliance-owned Campa Cola in 2023 with aggressive pricing (e.g., ₹10 packs) is challenging Coca-Cola to adapt its strategies and potentially re-evaluate pricing. Campa Cola has reportedly gained over 10% market share in the sparkling beverage category in select states and is projected to achieve a turnover of ₹1,000 crore in FY 2024-25.

flags.title: High and Inconsistent Dividend Payouts
flags.description: The company's dividend payout history shows significant inconsistency and large amounts. An interim dividend of INR 1,19,818.33 Lakhs was declared in FY 2023-24, followed by no dividend in FY 2022-23, and then INR 35,945.50 Lakhs in FY 2024-25. Such substantial and fluctuating payouts, particularly the very high figure in FY 2023-24, could potentially strain retained earnings and internal capital available for operations or future investments, especially given the observed profitability volatility.

flags.title: Strong Parent Company Support and Robust Brand Portfolio
flags.description: Coca-Cola India benefits significantly from being a wholly-owned subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, USA. The ultimate parent company reported strong global organic revenue growth (6% in Q3 2025 and 10% in Q1 2026) and expanding operating margins, indicating robust financial health. This provides substantial backing for the Indian operations. Furthermore, Coca-Cola India maintains a strong brand portfolio, including popular local brands like Thums Up, Maaza, and Limca, which contribute to its market strength.

flags.title: Consistent Commitment to Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR)
flags.description: Coca-Cola India demonstrates a strong and consistent commitment to Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives, regularly meeting or exceeding its required 2% of average net profits obligations. Key focus areas for FY 2024-25 include Water Conservation (Lake Rejuvenation), Agriculture (Fruit Circular Economy including Project Unnati), Waste Management, and Livelihood Enhancement Projects. This proactive approach to social and environmental stewardship can enhance brand reputation and mitigate certain operational risks.

Market share growth vs. A&SP growth.period_date: Fiscal Year 2025 (ended March 31, 2025) compared to FY24
Market share growth vs. A&SP growth.source: BestMediaInfo.com, Exchange4Media, Business Standard, The Economic Times, Social Samosa, The Financial Express, Retail India News

Market share growth vs. A&SP growth.value: In Fiscal Year 2025, Coca-Cola India's Advertising & Sales Promotion (A&SP) expenses decreased by 13.8% year-on-year. Concurrently, as of November 2025, Coca-Cola experienced a 10% decline in its market share in the Indian beverage industry. This suggests a correlation where a reduction in A&SP spend coincided with a decrease in market share.

data.Water usage ratio (liters of water per l: 1.78 liters of water per liter of beverage produced
data.Wastewater treatment capacity utilizatio: 100% of wastewater treated. Additionally, some bottling facilities in Jammu achieved 99% water recovery and Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) in December 2025.
data.ESG rating (e.g., MSCI, Sustainalytics): 'AA' (MSCI ESG rating for Coca-Cola HBC, a major bottler)

key regional approvals/certifications.certification: Compliance with MCA mandate for dematerialization
key regional approvals/certifications.details: Coca-Cola India officially admitted its equity shares for dematerialization into NSDL on May 23, 2024, in compliance with India's Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) mandate for private companies, under ISIN INE0X2U01015.

key regional approvals/certifications.certification: Alliance for Water Stewardship (AWS) Gold level certification
key regional approvals/certifications.details: The Coca-Cola India Private Limited (CCIPL) Facility in Pirangut Village, Mulshi Taluk, Pune, Maharashtra, India, was recommended for Gold level certification against the AWS International Water Stewardship Standard Version 2, following an audit conducted from December 12-15, 2022.

Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverage Pvt Ltd (HCCB) bottling plant in Bidadi Industrial Area, Ramanagara, Karnataka, achieved carbon neutral certification according to the international standard PAS 2060. This certification by DNV was to be issued in May 2024, marking it as the first Coca-Cola bottling plant in India and the Southwest Asia region to achieve this milestone.
The Concentrate Plant Laboratory of Coca-Cola India Pvt. Ltd. is accredited with ISO/IEC 17025:2017 "General Requirements for the Competence of Testing" by NABL.

key regional approvals/certifications.certification: FSSAI (Food Safety and Standards Authority of India)
key regional approvals/certifications.details: Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Private Limited (HCCB) complies with FSSAI regulations, which are the national authority for regulating and supervising food safety in India.
HCCB adopts FSSC 22000, a globally recognized certification scheme for the food and beverage sector.
HCCB adopts ISO 9001:2015 for quality management.
Applied in production plants to reduce environmental impact, such as efficient water use and packaging recycling.
Applied for worker safety.
Coca-Cola's India Global Capability Center (GCC) has been recognized with awards including the National Water Award, Global CSR & Sustainability Award, and Greentech CSR India Award.

regional revenue trend.contribution_to_global_unit_case_volume_: India was a key market contributing to The Coca-Cola Company's global unit case volume growth of 3%.

regional revenue trend.market_position: Fifth-largest market for The Coca-Cola Company

focus.value: Largest franchisee of PepsiCo in the world (outside USA), producing and distributing carbonated soft drinks, non-carbonated drinks, and packaged water under PepsiCo trademarks (e.g., Pepsi, 7UP, Mirinda, Mountain Dew, Tropicana Juices). Also expanding internationally, particularly in Africa.
Diversified FMCG with a strong presence in confectionery, powdered and liquid beverages (NESCAFÉ), prepared dishes (MAGGI), and pet food.
Broad FMCG portfolio including home care, beauty & personal care, and foods & refreshment, which encompasses various beverage products.
Health drinks and fruit-based beverages (Real brand), Ayurvedic products, home and personal care.

channel breakdown (B2B/B2C, direct/distr.B2B Sales (Concentrate and Beverage Base: Coca-Cola India Private Limited manufactures and sells concentrate and beverage bases to its bottling partners, authorized bottling and canning operators, distributors, fountain wholesalers, and fountain retailers.

B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.overall_model: Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages (HCCB) and other authorized bottling partners are responsible for preparing, packaging, selling, and distributing finished beverages.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.distribution_network: Multi-level distribution network including factories, bottling plants, warehouses, stockists, wholesalers, and retailers.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.distributor_model: HCCB delivers its beverages to over 2,000+ distributors nationwide and distributes through FMCG wholesalers to clusters of villages.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.direct_distribution_urban: In urban areas, products are distributed directly from bottling plants to retailers.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.hub_and_spoke_model_rural: For remote rural villages with poor infrastructure, a three-tier 'hub and spoke' distribution model is employed.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.digital_e_commerce: Actively engaging in digital commerce, piloting hyperlocal delivery via 'Hut to Home' models and strengthening tie-ups with platforms like Blinkit, Swiggy, and JioMart.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.eb2b_platform: An AI-enabled digital platform, 'Coke Buddy,' is used by more than 10 lakh (1 million) retailers for placing orders, tracking deliveries, and accessing promotions.
B2C Distribution (Finished Beverages) vi.diverse_channels: Key customers are also served across diverse channels including Modern Trade, Food Services & On-Premise, Travel, and Government Institutions.

strengths: Achieved significant revenue growth from INR 229,751.11 Lakhs in FY 2020-21 to INR 504,255.69 Lakhs in FY 2024-25.
Demonstrates strong capital efficiency with a Return on Equity (ROE) of 42.94% for FY 2024-25.
Holds a dominant market share of 60% in carbonated drinks and 71% in the diet and light category as of FY25.
Possesses an extensive distribution network reaching 4.5 million stores and deploying close to 60,000 units of eutectic chest coolers, covering 41% of urban and 32% of rural households.
Benefits from a strong brand portfolio, including Thums Up, India's first billion-dollar beverage brand, and the parent company's global brand value of $60.1 billion (7th globally in 2025).
weaknesses: Experienced significant fluctuations in profitability, with EBITDA dropping from INR 105,948.34 Lakhs in FY 2022-23 to INR 67,523.15 Lakhs in FY 2023-24.
Over 50% of revenue comes from Carbonated Soft Drinks (CSD), while its Non-Alcoholic Ready-To-Drink (NARTD) segment experienced a decline in Q1 2026.
Incurs substantial royalty payments, amounting to 11.04% of Net Sales/Revenue in FY2025 to its parent company.
Has a history of regulatory issues, including a transfer pricing dispute ruling in 2009 and significant related party transactions (RPTs) that require scrutiny.
Vulnerable to rising input costs, such as a 17.19% increase in PET resin prices in India (Q1 2026) and a 29.83% increase in global aluminum can prices (last 12 months).
opportunities: Operates in the India Non-Alcoholic Beverages Market, projected to grow at a CAGR of 7.94% (2026-2034) to USD 34.71 Billion by 2025.
Has successfully grown its zero-sugar portfolio to 30% of total volumes in 2025, indicating potential for further expansion in health-conscious segments (e.g., Powerade launch in March 2026).
Significant opportunity to expand distribution in untapped rural markets, currently covering only 32% of rural households compared to 41% of urban households.
The exploration of a potential public listing (IPO) for Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings Pvt. Ltd. (HCCH) in 2027 could unlock capital and strategic advantages.
Strong existing focus with over 65% of total media spend dedicated to digital marketing in 2025, allowing for deeper consumer engagement and targeted campaigns.
threats: Faces intense competition from peers like Varun Beverages (21.51% EBITDA margin, Q1 FY26) and PepsiCo India, contributing to a 10% decline in carbonated drinks market share in 2025.
Incurred INR 50.66 Crores in environmental non-compliance fines and faces ongoing regulatory requirements (e.g., MCA mandate for dematerialization).
Continued global increases in key raw materials like aluminum cans (up 29.83% in 12 months) and PET resin (up 17.19% in India, Q1 2026) pose a threat to cost structures.
Operates solely in India, exposing the company to country-specific economic downturns, policy changes, and political instability without geographic diversification.
Despite strong CSD sales, the decline in Coca-Cola India's NARTD segment in Q1 2026 suggests a potential threat from consumers shifting away from traditional CSDs towards healthier alternatives.

executives + backgrounds.name: Sanket Ray
executives + backgrounds.background: Joined the company in 2004 and was named to his current position in 2020. Prior to this, he served as the Chief Operating Officer for mainland China.

executives + backgrounds.name: Harsh Bhutani
executives + backgrounds.role: Chief Financial Officer (CFO), Coca-Cola India & Southwest Asia
executives + backgrounds.background: A seasoned professional with over 24 years of experience across various finance domains, including business strategy. He is a Commerce Graduate from Delhi University and a qualified Chartered Accountant.

executives + backgrounds.name: Devyani Rajya Laxmi Rana
executives + backgrounds.role: Vice President – Public Affairs, Communications and Sustainability, Coca-Cola India & South West Asia
executives + backgrounds.background: Possesses over 25 years of experience in government and corporate affairs, strategic business advocacy, risk management, communication excellence, corporate social responsibility, and sustainability.

executives + backgrounds.name: Arnab Roy
executives + backgrounds.role: Vice President, Marketing, Coca-Cola India and South West Asia
executives + backgrounds.background: Has been with The Coca-Cola Company for over 20 years, with extensive experience in brand building with an integrated business mindset, and has led marketing teams across multiple geographies and cultures.

executives + backgrounds.name: Ambuj Deo Singh
executives + backgrounds.role: Vice President, O2O (Offline to Online), Coca-Cola India and Southwest Asia
executives + backgrounds.background: Has successfully held numerous roles in business operations, customer management, and consumer marketing during his more than 15 years at Coca-Cola India. He is responsible for directing the organization's digital agenda across customer and consumer verticals.

board members + roles.role: Director (specific role within the operating unit not detailed in public leadership pages)

litigation status.status: An Indian court ruled in 2009 that Coca-Cola India failed to apply transfer pricing rules following an agreement to offer advisory services to a related party.

Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages (HCCB), a bottling partner, faced a long-standing legal challenge in Kerala regarding water scarcity and environmental pollution at its Plachimada plant. HCCB renounced its license in July 2017 and informed the Supreme Court that it would not resume operations at that facility.

In December 2024, the Bombay High Court refused to quash criminal proceedings initiated against Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Private Limited 14 years prior, related to a 2001 case concerning adulterated Canada Dry beverages.

The Coca-Cola Company (KO), the ultimate parent company, is preparing for a legal confrontation in the U.S. regarding a potential $20 billion tax dispute with the IRS. This dispute, concerning profit allocation among its international subsidiaries for tax years 2010-2025, is scheduled to be presented to the 11th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in June 2026.

data.RPT flags: Yes, significant related party transactions were executed at arm's length during FY 2024-25. These include transactions with Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Private Limited (Fellow Subsidiary) for sale/purchase of goods, discounts, marketing support, and rent paid. Additionally, royalty paid to, and receipt/provision of services with, The Coca-Cola Company, USA (Ultimate Parent Company) were reported.
data.ESG initiatives: The company demonstrates a strong commitment to Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives, consistently meeting or exceeding its required 2% of average net profits obligations. Key focus areas for FY 2024-25 include Water Conservation (e.g., Lake Rejuvenation across India), Agriculture (e.g., Fruit Circular Economy including Project Unnati), Waste Management (e.g., Waste Free Activation of high footfall locations), and Livelihood Enhancement Projects (e.g., Superpower Retailers' program, Contribution towards Sports and Athlete Development).

lead investors.value: Jubilant Bhartia Group, Goldman Sachs Asset Management (provided funding to Jubilant Bhartia Group)

amount raised.value: INR 117.044 billion (approximately $1.47 billion) for a 40% stake in Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings Pvt. Ltd. (HCCH). Goldman Sachs Asset Management provided $600 million in funding to Jubilant Bhartia Group for this acquisition.

round/stage.value: Initial Public Offering (IPO) of Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings Pvt. Ltd. (HCCH)

lead investors.value: Public investors (The Coca-Cola Company to sell a portion of its shareholding)

amount raised.value: Expected to raise over $1 billion, with a potential valuation of HCCH over $10 billion.

round/stage.value: Capital Investment by The Coca-Cola System

amount raised.value: Additional US$3 billion through 2020, bringing total planned investment from 2012-2020 to US$5 billion. Total investment since re-entry in 1993 reached US$7 billion with this announcement.

target.value: Parle's beverage business (including brands Thums Up, Limca, Gold Spot)

strategic rationale.value: To quickly rebuild market position in India after re-entry and capture over 50% of India's beverage market overnight.

upcoming catalysts — each with date/quar.event: The Coca-Cola Company is exploring a potential public listing (IPO) in India for Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings Pvt. Ltd. (HCCH), the parent company of its largest bottler, Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Pvt. Ltd. (HCCB). This potential listing is subject to market conditions and regulatory approvals, with initial preparations underway for listing on the Bombay Stock Exchange and National Stock Exchange of India. The company is also considering selling a portion of its shareholding in HCCH in connection with the listing.
upcoming catalysts — each with date/quar.materiality: High. This would be a significant milestone, completing the refranchising of HCCH and positioning it to capitalize on opportunities in the Indian market. It could also be one of India's biggest FMCG IPOs ever, potentially valuing the bottling business at around ₹85,000 crore ($10 billion).

corporate milestones — each with date an.event: Dhurandhar achieved a massive television premiere, reaching over 53 million viewers on Star Gold, Star Gold 2, and Colors Cineplex.
Coca-Cola India launched its first-ever activation at BLR Airport, blending digital convenience with refreshment.
Powerade entered India as the Official Sports Drink of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026.
Coca-Cola India expanded its presence at Magh Mela 2026, building on its successful participation at Maha Kumbh 2025, focusing on hydration, accessibility, and local employment.

Coca-Cola India, along with its bottling partner Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Pvt. Ltd., strengthened its commitment to local ecosystems through large-scale hydration access, retailer enablement, and community-led interventions at Medaram Jatara 2026, Asia's largest tribal congregation.
Coca-Cola marked a landmark moment with the launch of the first-ever Coke Studio Bharat LIVE, debuting across Delhi and Guwahati.

Thums Up, a brand acquired by Coca-Cola, became a billion-dollar brand.
An interim dividend of 230% (Rs. 23 per Equity Share) was paid, resulting in an outgo of Rs. 45,930.36 Lakhs.
An interim dividend of 400% (Rs. 40 per Equity Share) was paid, resulting in an outgo of Rs. 79,878.89 Lakhs.
Sanket Ray was named President of India and Southwest Asia for The Coca-Cola Company.
A dividend amounting to Rs. 63,903.11 Lakhs was paid, along with a Dividend Distribution Tax of Rs. 13,135.47 Lakhs.
Coca-Cola acquired Parle's portfolio of brands, including Thums Up, Limca, and Gold Spot, which were leading soft drink brands in India.
Coca-Cola re-entered the Indian market after a 16-year absence, benefiting from new liberalization policies.
Coca Cola India Private Limited was established in Mulshi, Maharashtra, India, with CIN: U15549PN1992FTC065522. It was formerly known as Britco Foods Company Limited.
Coca-Cola withdrew its operations from India due to government regulations and legislation that limited the dilution of equity of multinational corporations and required formula disclosure.
Coca-Cola first entered India, operating with 100% foreign equity.
Coca-Cola was first introduced in India, and its first factory was established in New Delhi.

key regional approvals/certifications.certification: Compliance with MCA mandate for dematerialization
key regional approvals/certifications.details: Coca-Cola India officially admitted its equity shares for dematerialization into NSDL on May 23, 2024, in compliance with India's Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) mandate for private companies, under ISIN INE0X2U01015.

key regional approvals/certifications.certification: Alliance for Water Stewardship (AWS) Gold level certification
key regional approvals/certifications.details: The Coca-Cola India Private Limited (CCIPL) Facility in Pirangut Village, Mulshi Taluk, Pune, Maharashtra, India, was recommended for Gold level certification against the AWS International Water Stewardship Standard Version 2, following an audit conducted from December 12-15, 2022.

Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverage Pvt Ltd (HCCB) bottling plant in Bidadi Industrial Area, Ramanagara, Karnataka, achieved carbon neutral certification according to the international standard PAS 2060. This certification by DNV was to be issued in May 2024, marking it as the first Coca-Cola bottling plant in India and the Southwest Asia region to achieve this milestone.
The Concentrate Plant Laboratory of Coca-Cola India Pvt. Ltd. is accredited with ISO/IEC 17025:2017 "General Requirements for the Competence of Testing" by NABL.

key regional approvals/certifications.certification: FSSAI (Food Safety and Standards Authority of India)
key regional approvals/certifications.details: Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages Private Limited (HCCB) complies with FSSAI regulations, which are the national authority for regulating and supervising food safety in India.
HCCB adopts FSSC 22000, a globally recognized certification scheme for the food and beverage sector.
HCCB adopts ISO 9001:2015 for quality management.
Applied in production plants to reduce environmental impact, such as efficient water use and packaging recycling.
Applied for worker safety.
Coca-Cola's India Global Capability Center (GCC) has been recognized with awards including the National Water Award, Global CSR & Sustainability Award, and Greentech CSR India Award.

targets.target: Invest INR 350 Cr in bottler capex
